description_long: This object is a classic regulatory stop sign, featuring a red octagonal face with a bold white border and the word 'STOP' in large, uppercase white letters at its center. The sign is mounted on a tall, cylindrical metallic pole, which is vertically oriented and appears sturdy enough for outdoor installation. The sign face is flat and thin, with a slight thickness visible from the side and rear views, and the pole extends well below the sign, suggesting it is designed to be embedded into the ground. The rear of the sign is a muted gray, likely unpainted metal, and lacks any text or markings. The materials appear to be primarily metal for the pole and backing, with a reflective coated surface for the sign face to ensure visibility in various lighting conditions. This stop sign is typical of those found at intersections in urban and suburban environments, serving as a clear traffic control device.
description: A red octagonal stop sign with white border and 'STOP' text, mounted on a tall metallic pole. The sign is reflective and designed for outdoor use at intersections.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red octagonal sign face with white 'STOP' text and border, mounted on a gray pole.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the sign face and the cylindrical pole, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain gray back of the sign and the full length of the pole.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the sign and the pole, with no visible text.
